mnemonics.title: Virtual Function Rules
mnemonics.content: P.A.V.E.: Pure (virtual void func() = 0), Abstract (class becomes abstract), Virtual (use virtual keyword), Override (use override specifier)

mnemonics.title: Virtual Destructor Importance
mnemonics.content: Remember: D.E.L.E.T.E. - Destructors Ensure Lifetime End Through Every Type (polymorphic deletion)

mnemonics.title: VTable Memory
mnemonics.content: V.P.T.R. = Virtual Pointer To Runtime dispatch - Each polymorphic object has a V-PTR pointing to V-TABLE containing function pointers

mnemonics.title: Abstract Class Characteristics
mnemonics.content: A.B.C. = Abstract Base Cannot (instantiate) - Abstract classes cannot be instantiated but can have constructors

acronyms.expansion: Virtual Table - lookup table for virtual function resolution
acronyms.usage: Each class with virtual functions has a vtable containing pointers to the most-derived virtual functions

acronyms.expansion: Virtual Pointer - hidden pointer in objects pointing to class vtable
acronyms.usage: The vptr is initialized in constructor and used at runtime to find the correct function
dynamic_cast and typeid require RTTI for runtime type information

acronyms.expansion: = 0 syntax making function abstract with no implementation
acronyms.usage: Used to create abstract base classes that define interface without implementation

visualTips.visualization: Draw boxes: [Object1] with vptr → [vtable: func1→&Derived1::func, func2→&Base::func], [Object2] with vptr → [vtable: func1→&Base::func]
Draw arrow from object → vptr → vtable → actual function address - shows runtime dispatch path
Draw [Base] + [Derived] = [Base] only - show derived portion 'sliced off' when assigned by value
Draw trapezoid (abstract) → rectangle (concrete) - shows IS-A relationship with pure virtual as contract

analogies.analogy: Like a TV remote with different brand buttons - the same button (function call) performs different actions (implementations) based on the TV (object type) you point it at
Like a restaurant menu - each table (object) has a waiter (vptr) who consults the master menu (vtable) to know what dish (function) to bring for each order
Like a job posting (abstract requirement) - defines what skills are needed but doesn't implement them; subclasses must provide actual implementations
Like having a master key that can open any lock - ensures all locks (destructors from derived to base) are properly closed when cleaning up
